title: Use arithmetic sequence formulas
description: Given the formula of an arithmetic sequence, either in explicit form or in recursive form, find a specific term in the sequence.

assessment_items.question_content:
$a(n) = -5 + 6(n - 1)$

**Find the $12^\text{th}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is an explicit formula.

All we have to do is plug $n=12$ in the formula to find the $12^\text{th}$ term.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}a(1)=20\\\\
a(n)=a(n-1)-17
\end{cases}$

**Find the $3^{\text{rd}}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $20$ and that the common difference is $-17$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}b(1)=-7\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)+12
\end{cases}$

**Find the $4^{\text{th}}$ term in the sequence.**


[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $-7$ and that the common difference is $12$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}c(1)=-20\\\\
c(n)=c(n-1)+10
\end{cases}$

**Find the $2^{\text{nd}}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $-20$ and that the common difference is $10$.
$\begin{align}
\blueD{c(1)}&=-20
\\\\
\maroonD{c(2)}&=\blueD{c(1)}+10=-10
\end{align}$
The $2^{\text{nd}}$ term is $-10$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}d(1)=3\\\\
d(n)=d(n-1)-14
\end{cases}$

**Find the $3^{\text{rd}}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $3$ and that the common difference is $-14$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}a(1)=-2\\\\
a(n)=a(n-1)-5
\end{cases}$

**Find the $4^{\text{th}}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $-2$ and that the common difference is $-5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}b(1)=16\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)+1
\end{cases}$

**Find the $2^{\text{nd}}$ term in the sequence.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $16$ and that the common difference is $1$.
$\begin{align}
\blueD{b(1)}&=16
\\\\
\maroonD{b(2)}&=\blueD{b(1)}+1=17
\end{align}$
The $2^{\text{nd}}$ term is $17$.
